sentences.base_language: My name is Sofía. I live in a small apartment near the city centre.
sentences.target_language: 我叫索菲亚。我住在市中心附近的一间小公寓里。
sentences.romanized: Wǒ jiào Suǒfēiyà. Wǒ zhù zài shì zhōngxīn fùjìn de yī jiān xiǎo gōngyù lǐ.
sentences.grammarNote: 叫 (jiào) = to be called. 我叫 is the standard self-introduction. 住在 (zhù zài) = live at/in. The pattern 在...里 (zài...lǐ) = inside of: 在公寓里 (inside the apartment).

sentences.base_language: Every morning I wake up, eat bread with butter and drink coffee.
sentences.target_language: 每天早上，我起床，吃黄油面包，喝咖啡。
sentences.romanized: Měi tiān zǎoshang, wǒ qǐchuáng, chī huángyóu miànbāo, hē kāfēi.
sentences.grammarNote: 起床 (qǐchuáng) = get up (out of bed). 每天 (měi tiān) = every day. Note the verb choice: 吃 (chī) = eat, 喝 (hē) = drink. Chinese uses different verbs for eating vs drinking, never interchangeably.

sentences.base_language: I am happy — or at least, I feel calm.
sentences.target_language: 我很快乐——或者至少，我感到平静。
sentences.romanized: Wǒ hěn kuàilè — huòzhě zhìshǎo, wǒ gǎndào píngjìng.
sentences.grammarNote: 很 (hěn) normally means 'very', but before adjectives in simple statements it's often a linking word with little emphasis: 我很好 = I'm fine (not necessarily 'very good'). 感到 (gǎndào) = to feel / to sense.

sentences.base_language: Today is a good day. I need to go to the market near my neighbourhood.
sentences.target_language: 今天是美好的一天。我需要去我家附近的市场。
sentences.romanized: Jīntiān shì měihǎo de yī tiān. Wǒ xūyào qù wǒ jiā fùjìn de shìchǎng.
sentences.grammarNote: Adjectives in Chinese come before the noun they modify, connected by 的 (de): 美好的一天 (a wonderful day), 附近的市场 (the nearby market). The structure is: [modifier] + 的 + [noun].

sentences.base_language: I put on my coat and walk straight ahead, then turn left at the corner.
sentences.target_language: 我穿上外套，一直走，然后在街角向左转。
sentences.romanized: Wǒ chuān shàng wàitào, yīzhí zǒu, ránhòu zài jiējiǎo xiàng zuǒ zhuǎn.
sentences.grammarNote: 穿上 (chuān shàng) = put on clothing. The complement 上 (shàng) after a verb marks completion or attachment: 穿上 (put on), 关上 (close/shut), 锁上 (lock). 一直 (yīzhí) = straight / continuously.

sentences.base_language: The street is busy but not too noisy. The sun is hot on my face and shoulder.
sentences.target_language: 街道很热闹，但不太嘈杂。阳光照在我的脸和肩膀上，很热。
sentences.romanized: Jiēdào hěn rènào, dàn bù tài cáozá. Yángguāng zhào zài wǒ de liǎn hé jiānbǎng shàng, hěn rè.
sentences.grammarNote: 热闹 (rènào) = lively / bustling (positive). 嘈杂 (cáozá) = noisy / chaotic (negative). Chinese often has paired words where the meaning subtly shifts: 热闹 implies energy and life, 嘈杂 implies unpleasant noise.

sentences.base_language: At the market, an old man named Paulo is a chef. He is also a musician — on weekends he plays his guitar in the park.
sentences.target_language: 在市场上，一位叫保罗的老人是厨师。他也是音乐家——周末在公园里弹吉他。
sentences.romanized: Zài shìchǎng shàng, yī wèi jiào Bǎoluó de lǎorén shì chúshī. Tā yě shì yīnyuèjiā — zhōumò zài gōngyuán lǐ tán jítā.
sentences.grammarNote: 位 (wèi) is a polite measure word for people. Compare: 一个人 (a person, neutral) vs 一位老人 (an elderly gentleman, respectful). 弹 (tán) is the verb for string instruments and piano; 吹 (chuī) is for wind instruments.

sentences.base_language: Today he is cooking soup with onion, tomato, and fresh herbs. It smells delicious.
sentences.target_language: 今天他在用洋葱、西红柿和新鲜香草煮汤。闻起来很好吃。
sentences.romanized: Jīntiān tā zài yòng yángcōng, xīhóngshì hé xīnxiān xiāngcǎo zhǔ tāng. Wén qǐlái hěn hǎochī.
sentences.grammarNote: 在 (zài) before a verb signals an ongoing action: 他在煮汤 = he is cooking soup. 闻起来 (wén qǐlái) = smells like. The 起来 (qǐlái) pattern describes sensory impressions: 看起来 (looks like), 听起来 (sounds like), 尝起来 (tastes like).

sentences.base_language: "Good morning!" he says. "Would you like to taste this?" "Yes, please." The soup is hot, a little spicy, and very tasty.
sentences.target_language: 他说：
我说：
汤很热，有点辣，非常好吃。
sentences.romanized: Tā shuō: 'Zǎoshang hǎo! Nǐ xiǎng cháng yī cháng ma?' Wǒ shuō: 'Hǎo de, xièxiè.' Tāng hěn rè, yǒudiǎn là, fēicháng hǎochī.
sentences.grammarNote: 尝一尝 (cháng yī cháng) = have a little taste. Repeating a verb with 一 (V一V) softens the action: 看一看 (have a quick look), 想一想 (think it over), 走一走 (take a little walk). It implies brevity or casualness.

sentences.base_language: I buy a big bowl and also some fruit — an apple, a banana, and an orange. I put them in my bag.
sentences.target_language: 我买了一大碗，还有一些水果——一个苹果、一根香蕉和一个橙子。我把它们放进包里。
sentences.romanized: Wǒ mǎile yī dà wǎn, hái yǒu yīxiē shuǐguǒ — yīgè píngguǒ, yī gēn xiāngjiāo hé yīgè chéngzi. Wǒ bǎ tāmen fàng jìn bāo lǐ.
sentences.grammarNote: Measure words: 一个 (yīgè) is the general measure word (apple, orange), but 一根 (yī gēn) is for long, thin objects: bananas, chopsticks, candles, strings. 把 (bǎ) construction: 我把它们放进包里 = I put them into the bag (emphasis on acting on the object).

sentences.base_language: Near the market there is a small café. I enter and sit at a table by the window.
sentences.target_language: 市场附近有一家小咖啡馆。我进去，在窗边的桌子旁坐下。
sentences.romanized: Shìchǎng fùjìn yǒu yī jiā xiǎo kāfēiguǎn. Wǒ jìnqù, zài chuāng biān de zhuōzi páng zuòxià.
sentences.grammarNote: Location + 有 + object = there is/are [something] at [place]. 市场附近有咖啡馆 (near the market there is a café). 家 (jiā) is the measure word for businesses and establishments. 坐下 (zuòxià) = sit down (下 signals downward completion).

sentences.base_language: A young waiter comes over. His name is Marcos. He is a student at the university and works here part-time.
sentences.target_language: 一位年轻的服务员走过来。他叫马科斯，是大学生，在这里兼职工作。
sentences.romanized: Yī wèi niánqīng de fúwùyuán zǒu guòlái. Tā jiào Mǎkēsī, shì dàxuéshēng, zài zhèlǐ jiānzhí gōngzuò.
sentences.grammarNote: 走过来 (zǒu guòlái) = walk over here (towards the speaker). Directional complements show movement: 过来 (guòlái, come over), 过去 (guòqù, go over there), 进来 (jìnlái, come in), 出去 (chūqù, go out).

sentences.base_language: "What do you recommend?" I ask. He thinks for a moment and smiles. "The cheese sandwich is very good today. And the orange juice."
sentences.target_language: 我问：
他想了一下，笑了。
sentences.romanized: Wǒ wèn: 'Nǐ tuījiàn shénme?' Tā xiǎngle yīxià, xiào le. 'Jīntiān de nǎilào sānmíngzhì hěn hǎo. Hái yǒu chéngjuē.'
sentences.grammarNote: 想了一下 (xiǎngle yīxià) = thought for a moment. 了 marks a completed action. 一下 (yīxià) softens or abbreviates the action: 等一下 (wait a moment), 看一下 (take a quick look), 说一下 (say a few words).

sentences.base_language: While I wait, I look out the window. A police officer is crossing the street. Two children are running in the park.
sentences.target_language: 等待的时候，我向窗外望去。一名警察正在过马路。两个孩子在公园里跑步。
sentences.romanized: Děngdài de shíhòu, wǒ xiàng chuāng wài wàng qù. Yī míng jǐngchá zhèngzài guò mǎlù. Liǎng gè háizi zài gōngyuán lǐ pǎobù.
sentences.grammarNote: 正在 (zhèngzài) = right now, in the middle of. It's a stronger marker of ongoing action than just 在: 他在吃饭 (he's eating) vs 他正在吃饭 (he's eating right at this moment). 名 (míng) is the measure word for people in formal/official roles.

sentences.base_language: I feel something warm — not just the coffee in my hands, but something deeper. Joy, maybe. Or comfort.
sentences.target_language: 我感受到一种温暖——不只是手中的咖啡，而是更深层的东西。也许是喜悦，或者是安慰。
sentences.romanized: Wǒ gǎnshòu dào yī zhǒng wēnnuǎn — bù zhǐ shì shǒu zhōng de kāfēi, ér shì gèng shēncéng de dōngxi. Yěxǔ shì xǐyuè, huòzhě shì ānwèi.
sentences.grammarNote: 不只是...而是... (bù zhǐ shì...ér shì...) = not just... but... This contrast pattern redirects rather than adds. Compare: 不只是...也是... (not just... also...) which adds. 种 (zhǒng) is the measure word for types or kinds.

sentences.base_language: After my meal, I leave the café and walk to the library. I love the library — it is quiet and full of good things.
sentences.target_language: 吃完饭后，我离开咖啡馆，走向图书馆。我爱图书馆。那里安静，充满了美好的事物。
sentences.romanized: Chī wán fàn hòu, wǒ líkāi kāfēiguǎn, zǒu xiàng túshūguǎn. Wǒ ài túshūguǎn. Nàlǐ ānjìng, chōngmǎnle měihǎo de shìwù.
sentences.grammarNote: 吃完 (chī wán) = finish eating. 完 (wán) as a result complement means 'to completion': 做完 (finish doing), 写完 (finish writing), 看完 (finish watching/reading). It's one of the most useful verb complements in Chinese.

sentences.base_language: I am learning a new language — it is not always easy, and sometimes I feel stress, but I am calm today.
sentences.target_language: 我在学一门新语言——并不总是容易，有时我感到压力，但今天我很平静。
sentences.romanized: Wǒ zài xué yī mén xīn yǔyán — bìng bù zǒng shì róngyì, yǒushí wǒ gǎndào yālì, dàn jīntiān wǒ hěn píngjìng.
sentences.grammarNote: 门 (mén) is the measure word for courses and languages: 一门语言 (a language), 一门课 (a course). 并不 (bìng bù) = not at all — a stronger negation than plain 不, emphasising the contrast with what one might expect.

sentences.base_language: On my way home, I pass the hospital on the corner. My friend Amara is a nurse there.
sentences.target_language: 回家的路上，我路过街角的医院。我的朋友阿马拉在那里是护士。
sentences.romanized: Huí jiā de lùshang, wǒ lùguò jiējiǎo de yīyuàn. Wǒ de péngyǒu Āmǎlā zài nàlǐ shì hùshi.

sentences.base_language: I arrive home just before dinner. I cook rice with chicken, a little salt, pepper, and oil.
sentences.target_language: 我在晚饭前刚好到家。我用鸡肉、少许盐、胡椒和油做米饭。
sentences.romanized: Wǒ zài wǎnfàn qián gānghǎo dào jiā. Wǒ yòng jīròu, shǎoxǔ yán, hújiāo hé yóu zuò mǐfàn.
sentences.grammarNote: 刚好 (gānghǎo) = just right / exactly / just in time. 用 (yòng) = to use / using. The pattern 用...做... (use [ingredient] to make [dish]) is the standard way to describe cooking with ingredients.

sentences.base_language: I eat alone, but I do not feel lonely. I think about Paulo and his soup, Marcos and his smile, Amara and her courage.
sentences.target_language: 我一个人吃饭，但我不感到孤独。我想到了保罗和他的汤，马科斯和他的笑容，阿马拉和她的勇气。
sentences.romanized: Wǒ yīgè rén chīfàn, dàn wǒ bù gǎndào gūdú. Wǒ xiǎngdào le Bǎoluó hé tā de tāng, Mǎkēsī hé tā de xiàoróng, Āmǎlā hé tā de yǒngqì.
sentences.grammarNote: 一个人 (yīgè rén) = by oneself / alone. 想到 (xiǎngdào) = to think of / to recall. The complement 到 (dào) marks that the thinking reached its object: 想到了 (thought of and arrived at the thought). Compare: 想 (to think/miss) vs 想到 (to think of something specific).

sentences.base_language: I feel grateful. Here, in my small apartment, in this busy city, I am home.
sentences.target_language: 我感到感激。在这里，在我的小公寓里，在这座繁忙的城市中，我在家。
sentences.romanized: Wǒ gǎndào gǎnjī. Zài zhèlǐ, zài wǒ de xiǎo gōngyù lǐ, zài zhè zuò fánmáng de chéngshì zhōng, wǒ zài jiā.
sentences.grammarNote: 座 (zuò) is the measure word for large, fixed structures: 一座城市 (a city), 一座山 (a mountain), 一座桥 (a bridge), 一座楼 (a building). Using the right measure word sounds natural; using 个 instead for these would sound unpolished.
